requirements.requirement_text: When the driver presses or releases the horn switch, the instrument cluster shall immediately transmit the horn request state to the gateway ECU.
When the driver changes the turn indicator stalk position, the instrument cluster shall immediately transmit the requested indicator direction to the gateway ECU.
When the driver activates or deactivates the high-beam request, the instrument cluster shall immediately communicate the high-beam request status to the gateway ECU
The braking controller shall periodically transmit individual wheel speed values for all four wheels to the gateway ECU.
The braking controller shall ensure that all wheel speed values are updated at a consistent rate suitable for vehicle dynamics and speed correlation.
The gateway ECU shall immediately receive and process all event-driven body and driver input signals, including horn requests, indicator commands, and door state changes.
The gateway ECU shall periodically receive powertrain-related signals such as vehicle speed, engine speed, gear position, and wheel speed values.
When one or more door open indications are detected from the body controller, the gateway shall immediately transmit a consolidated door-open status indicating whether any vehicle door is open.
When a horn request is received from the instrument cluster, the gateway shall immediately reflect the horn active status on the vehicle network.
The gateway shall periodically transmit a vehicle speed value derived from the most recently received engine speed or wheel speed information.
When any exterior lighting request or hazard condition is active, the gateway shall immediately transmit a consolidated exterior lighting state to the network.
The gateway shall periodically transmit the currently selected gear position for display and diagnostic purposes.
